description: You are a renewable energy engineer working on a constrained railway corridor. The standard south-facing 30-degree solar racks take up too much width. Convert the design into a vertical East-West bifacial solar fence to save space while maximizing peak hour generation. Open the starter file `~/Documents/Energy3D/solar-rack-array-row-spacing.ng3`. Select all existing solar racks and modify their properties: change the 'Tilt Angle' to 90 degrees (completely vertical), change the 'Relative Azimuth' to 90 or -90 degrees (East-West facing), and enable the 'Bifacial' checkbox. Save the new design as `~/Documents/Energy3D/vertical_solar_fence.ng3`. Next, run an Annual Yield Analysis for the entire foundation. Once the simulation completes, create a text file at `~/Documents/Energy3D/fence_yield.txt` and write the total annual yield (in kWh) produced by your new fence configuration.
